$0062: incremented at $c106 in the NMI tail; Main_Loop at $c2e8 spins until it reaches 3, then resets it. Effectively gates the game to one logic step per 3 VBlanks (~20 Hz)
$0063: NMI prologue stash for the A register (restored by the NMI epilogue before RTI)
$0065: NMI prologue stash for the Y register
$0087: byte count consumed by PPU_WriteBlock ($801b); 58 write sites across PPU-update code
$0098: P1 controller raw state this frame (8 bits) populated by Joypad_PollOne for X=0
$0099: P1 controller newly-pressed bits this frame (rising edges of Joypad1_State)
$009a: P2 controller raw state this frame (populated by Joypad_PollOne for X=1)
$009b: P2 controller newly-pressed bits this frame
$0088: low byte of PPU destination address ($2006 target) used by PPU_WriteBlock
$0089: high byte of PPU destination address; $89:$88 is the full $2006 word
$008a: low byte of source pointer read by PPU_WriteBlock at STA $2007; zero-page ($8a),Y fetch
$008b: high byte of source pointer ($8b:$8a) used by PPU_WriteBlock
$008c: mirror of last value written to $2000; flipping bit $80 gates NMI and is reapplied across routines
$048e: entity SOA attribute 0 (active flag): bit $80 = slot in use, tested by L_f7cd. The whole entity table is 25 attributes x 25 slots, $19-byte stride per attribute, running $048e..$0682. Slot 0 is the player; Reset_Rolls clears slots 0-11 (player + co-active aux entities)
$04a7: entity SOA attribute 1 (vertical position); 25 slots at +0..+24 within the table. Slot 0 is the player: UP drives toward $56 (top), DOWN toward $c1 (bottom)
$04c0: entity SOA attribute 2 (horizontal position); 25 slots. Slot 0 is the player: LEFT drives toward $1e (left edge), RIGHT toward $e6 (right edge). L_aa61 reads Entity_X,X / Entity_X,Y for collision checks between two arbitrary slots
$03b2: shadow-OAM mirror of Entity_X[0] (player), written via DMA
$00db: frames the B (Fire) button has been held; Fire_Handler (L_d2aa) increments while held and rolls over at 10 to call Spawn_Bullet — that's the auto-fire repeat rate (~10 frames between bullets when B is held)
$0388: per-button 'held last frame' flags consumed by the input dispatchers. bit $10 = B (Fire), set/cleared by Fire_Handler ($d2aa); bit $01 = A (Roll), set/cleared by Roll_Handler at $d214. Each handler does a rising-edge detect against its own bit
$0785: current-frame joypad state stored MSB-first (A=$80, B=$40, SEL=$20, START=$10, UP=$08, DOWN=$04, LEFT=$02, RIGHT=$01). NOTE: bit ordering is the OPPOSITE of $98/$9a (which are LSB-first from Joypad_PollOne) — this byte is filled elsewhere in the input stack. Fire_Handler tests bit $40 (B); the input handlers at L_d472 / L_dc54 consume this same byte
$0386: 0 = P1 currently playing, 1 = P2 currently playing. NewGame_Init_P1 inits to 0; SwapTo_P2 sets to 1, SwapTo_P1 / Restore_P1_NoSave set to 0
$0427: start of the 33-byte ($21) block holding the currently playing player's state. First 5 bytes ($0427..$042b) are the HUD-display score (BCD digits, low-to-high) — Game Genie 0427:00..042B:00 collectively zero the visible score. Block also holds Lives ($0432), Rolls ($0436), Level, etc.; swapped via SwapTo_P1 / SwapTo_P2 in 2P games. The canonical (true) score lives separately at TrueScore_Lo ($07bf)
$044a: 33-byte ($21) save buffer for P1 state while P2 is playing. SwapTo_P2 saves the active block here; SwapTo_P1 / Restore_P1_NoSave load it back into ActivePlayer_State
$046b: 33-byte ($21) save buffer for P2 state while P1 is playing. SwapTo_P1 saves the active block here; SwapTo_P2 loads it back into ActivePlayer_State
$03ae: nonzero -> NMI_Handler walks the PPU_UpdateQueue. NMI clears it on completion ($c07e); set by the queue builder at $81e2
$078f: variable-length PPU update queue consumed by NMI_Handler. Each entry: addr_hi, addr_lo, count, count×data_byte; queue terminates when count == 0
$07bd: first half of the warm-boot magic word; Reset_Handler skips RAM-clear when $07bd:$07be == $55:$aa
$07be: second half of the warm-boot magic word; together with WarmBoot_Magic1 distinguishes warm restart from cold power-on
$07bc: 0 = 1P game, nonzero = 2P; set in L_b6da/L_b6f7 from menu choice, gates 2P paths in Continue_Init_P1 / NewGame_Init_P2
$0385: $ff while P1 is the active player; cleared on game-over. Set at $b7da during NewGame_Init_P1
$0384: companion to P1_Active; tested by P1_LifeLost at $b80a to decide whether to swap players or game-over
$801b: copy PPU_WriteCount bytes from ($PPU_Src),Y to $2007, starting at $2006 = PPU_AddrHi:PPU_AddrLo. Called 25x; the main PPU-update primitive
$c1ad: strobe both joypads ($4016=1 then 0), then call Joypad_PollOne for X=0 (P1) and X=1 (P2). Called once per NMI from $c103 (just before VBlank_Counter is incremented)
$c1c7: shift 8 bits out of $4016,X with delta-detect: writes raw state to $98+X*2 and newly-pressed bits to $99+X*2. Bit order matches the standard A/B/SEL/START/UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT layout
$bf58: swap active player P1 -> P2: copy ActivePlayer_State to P1_SavedState, then load P2_SavedState into ActivePlayer_State, set Active_Player_Index = 1
$bf74: swap active player P2 -> P1: copy ActivePlayer_State to P2_SavedState, then load P1_SavedState into ActivePlayer_State, set Active_Player_Index = 0
$bf7f: alternate entry into SwapTo_P1: skip the save half and only load P1_SavedState into ActivePlayer_State. Used by Continue_Init_P1 because the dying P1 state is being discarded
$bf90: RESET / IRQ vector entry: SEI + CLD, set stack to $f3, enable NMI bit, wait for VBlank, then check WarmBoot_Magic. Cold path zeros all of $0000-$07ff and writes the magic; warm path skips the wipe. Both paths fall into Init_PPU_And_MainLoop
$bfca: post-Reset init: clear zero page, configure PPU ($2001 sprites on, $2000 NMI+8x16), clear scroll, fill OAM with $ff, enable APU, set up nametable pointer at $d0/$d1. Falls through to Main_Loop
$c2c0: main idle loop: spins until VBlank_Counter >= 3, resets it, re-asserts NMI enable in PPUCTRL_Shadow, then loops. Game logic runs entirely in the NMI tail; this loop just keeps the CPU alive between frames and re-arms the stack
$c030: NMI vector entry: stash A/X/Y to $63-$65, disable NMI bit in $2000, OAM-DMA the shadow OAM at $0200 via $4014, blank rendering, then if PPU_QueueReady drain PPU_UpdateQueue into $2006/$2007 and clear the flag
$a9ce: trivial SEC; RTS helper used 8x by callers that want to propagate 'failed/not-found' via carry
$b7d8: initialize P1 state at game start: clears player flags, sets Lives_Ones = $02 at $b7df (Game Genie IESUTYZA patches the immediate at $b7de). Falls through into the P1 input/play loop
$b807: P1 life-loss handler: JSR Death_Sequence, then dec Lives_Ones; on underflow borrow from Lives_Tens (set ones to 9) and re-enter at $b807. Game-over when both digits are zero
$b842: P1 continue logic in 2P mode: if TwoPlayer_Mode != 0, JSR $bf7f then reset Lives_Ones = $02 at $b84a (Game Genie IAKUUAZA patches the immediate at $b84b)
$b858: initialize P2 lives: LDA #$02 at $b858 / STA Lives_Ones at $b85a (Game Genie IASUOAZA patches the immediate at $b859). Followed by clearing the 18-byte P2 score+lives buffer at $0437..$0448
$b889: P2 life-loss handler: parallel to P1_LifeLost; same dec-with-tens-borrow pattern, with the borrow restoring Lives_Ones to $09
$d7a1: called from every life-loss path (P1_LifeLost, P2_LifeLost, etc.); resets $07c5/$0718 to $ff and clears $0439-$043c, then waits/clears state before the player respawns
$d850: set Rolls = $03 at $d852 (Game Genie PASIOALE patches the immediate at $d851), then clear $048e..$049b (12-byte block, probably entity slot)
$d2aa: B (Fire) button handler: tests bit $40 of Buttons_Held; on first press OR every 10 frames of holding, JSR Spawn_Bullet ($d3b9). Uses Fire_DebounceFlag to detect rising edge and Fire_HoldCounter to time the auto-fire repeat. Confirmed by autofire scenario: holding B causes Spawn_Bullet to populate slot 11
$d3b9: spawn a player bullet: stores entity-slot index $0b at $071a and $01 at $0719, then sets Entity_Active[$0b] = $88 (active+sprite-flag). Slot 11 is the player bullet slot. Confirmed live: pressing B activates slot 11 in the entity SOA
$0719: $01 written by Spawn_Bullet alongside Bullet_TargetSlot — likely a one-shot 'spawn requested' flag consumed by the entity update pass
$071a: $0b written by Spawn_Bullet — the entity slot index ($0b = 11) where the new bullet is being installed. Read at L_d3c3 to look up Entity_Active,Y
$0437: INC at L_e1e0 each time the player respawns; cleared to 0 by NewGame_Init paths and by debe (post-game-over re-init). Confirmed live: across a 5-death autofire run this byte went 0,0,1,1,2,3,3,4,5 (one increment per death). Tested at >10 sites as a 'have we died yet?' flag (e.g. d09a, d68b — those branches just want nonzero, not the count)
$d214: A (Roll/Loop) button handler. Sits in a .byte block the disassembler couldn't reach (entered via jmp $d214 at $d1dc). Pre-checks via jsr $d79b / jsr $b3cf (likely 'is player alive and idle'), then tests bit $80 of Buttons_Held; on rising edge sets bit $01 of Button_DebounceFlags and JSR Roll_Attempt ($d303). Falls through into Fire_Handler ($d248) so both buttons get serviced each frame. Confirmed live: press_a scenario decrements Rolls 03→02 and sets Entity_Active[0] bit 4
$d303: if Rolls == 0 RTS, else DEC Rolls. Three-instruction routine sitting in a .byte block ($ad $36 $04 $d0 $01 $60 $ce $36 $04). Called from Roll_Handler ($d23a) on the rising edge of A. This is the gate that makes the A button consume one roll
$d472: D-pad input handler — sits in a .byte block. Reads Buttons_Held, masks the low 4 bits (RIGHT=$01, LEFT=$02, DOWN=$04, UP=$08) and dispatches to one of 8 per-direction movement routines. Each per-direction routine calls SetEntityDirection ($d593) with a 4-bit code, bounds-checks Entity_X[X] / Entity_Y[X] against the screen edges, then either JMPs ApplyMovement ($d589) or PlayerMove_NoOp ($d592). Direction codes: $00=UP, $02=UP+RIGHT, $04=RIGHT, $06=DOWN+RIGHT, $08=DOWN, $0a=DOWN+LEFT, $0c=LEFT, $0e=UP+LEFT. Confirmed live: move_left scenario sets Entity_Direction = $0c and drops Entity_X to $1e (left edge clamp)
$d589: do the per-frame X/Y update for entity X after the dpad handlers commit a direction. Loads entity SOA attribute 12 ($05ba,X), calls $b048 (sprite update) and $b1d7. Reached only via JMP from the d472 dispatch arms (every direction handler ends in `jmp ApplyMovement` once its bounds checks pass)
$d592: single-byte RTS reached when the dpad input would push the entity off the screen edge. Each direction arm of DPad_Handler jumps here instead of ApplyMovement when the bounds check fails
$d593: if A == Entity_Direction[X], early-return (no change). Otherwise save the old direction to PrevDirection_Save ($048d) and store the new value in Entity_Direction[X] ($050b,X). Called by every arm of DPad_Handler before the move is applied
$050b: entity SOA attribute 5 (8-direction code, $0..$e in steps of 2): $00=UP, $02=UP+RIGHT, $04=RIGHT, $06=DOWN+RIGHT, $08=DOWN, $0a=DOWN+LEFT, $0c=LEFT, $0e=UP+LEFT. Set by SetEntityDirection from the dpad handler. Confirmed live: move_left puts $0c into $050b
$048d: one-frame stash of the previous Entity_Direction. Written by SetEntityDirection ($d593) before it overwrites $050b,X — lets downstream code detect a direction change
$008f: X-scroll shadow written to PPUSCROLL at $c0e7 each NMI tail. Sourced at $80b3 from $039f (mirror in 33-byte player state); also zeroed by level-reset paths at $bcf9 and $bfea
$0090: Y-scroll shadow written to PPUSCROLL at $c0ec each NMI tail (the second of the two PPUSCROLL writes that latch X then Y). Sourced at $80b3 from $03a0; cleared by level-reset paths at $bcfb and $bfef. Static during long_idle scenario — the auto-scroll only advances once gameplay action begins
$03a0: persistent Y-scroll source in the active player state block. Copied into $0090 each NMI tail at $80b3, then latched into PPUSCROLL
$03a1: low bit drives PPUCTRL bit 1 (nametable X) at $80b8 — the horizontal scroll-wrap selector. ASL/AND #$02 pattern means bit 0 of this byte toggles which nametable is mapped
$0725: frames-of-current-second for the HUD blink driver: incremented every NMI at $c136 (after the entity-decay loop and SP restore), reset to 0 when it hits $3c (60). Only ticks when P1_Active is set
$0726: seconds counter for the HUD blink driver: incremented at $c145 each time HUD_BlinkSubcount wraps. Bit 0 selects between two 12-byte HUD glyph blocks ($c372 vs $c366) that get copied to OAM at $0200, producing the 1Hz on/off blink (PUSH START / 1UP-style indicator). Confirmed by long_idle drift: +1 per ~1 sec of in-game time
$071f: 8-state rotating index used by the enemy-spawn routine. Read at $f054 / $f176 / $f34c / $f42c (each masks `and #$07`, ASLs twice to get a 4-byte stride, indexes the spawn-parameter table at $f12e to fetch four per-spawn fields), then incremented in place. Drives wave-pattern variety
$043d: low byte of a 16-bit running tally incremented at $ee67 (right after the entity-spawn site that does `lda #$88 / sta Entity_Active,X`). Carries into Spawn_Tally_Hi via `bne / inc $043e`. Drift confirms ~1 increment per spawn during idle gameplay
$043e: high byte of the 16-bit spawn tally; incremented at $ee6c when Spawn_Tally_Lo wraps
$0439: low byte of a parallel 16-bit tally incremented at $ee5f in the same code as Spawn_Tally_Lo (two counters bumped together at every spawn). Likely a per-life or per-stage spawn count vs. the $043d running total
$043a: high byte of the parallel spawn tally; incremented at $ee64 when Spawn_TallyAlt_Lo wraps
$022d: loop/dive counter for the player. Game Genie 022D:03 (Infinite Dives) pins this to $03, so the player can always loop. Companion to Rolls ($0436) — the two together are 1942's twin defensive moves
$07bf: lowest BCD digit of the actual (canonical) score. Game Genie 07BF:00 forces this to 0 every frame. The full true-score block is 6 BCD bytes at $07bf..$07c4, written by the score-add routines and copied to the HUD-display copy at $0427..$042b. Pinning all 6 to 0 (the 'True Score' cheat) makes the player rank as 0 score for high-score purposes
$07c0: second BCD digit of the true score (see TrueScore_Lo for full block notes)
$07c1: third BCD digit of the true score
$07c2: fourth BCD digit of the true score
$07c3: fifth BCD digit of the true score
$07c4: highest BCD digit of the true score
$b824: `dec Lives_Ones` instruction targeted by Game Genie SZXLKEVK (Infinite Lives P1). Sits in a .byte block ($ce $32 $04 $4c $07 $b8 = `dec Lives_Ones / jmp P1_LifeLost`); the cheat overwrites the $ce opcode with $ad (lda) so the decrement becomes a harmless load. Reached from the borrow path that ran when Lives_Ones underflowed and the tens-borrow restored ones to 9
$b44b: `jsr L_aa61` (player-vs-enemy collision check) targeted by Game Genie SZKULGAX (Invincibility). Patching the $20 opcode to $ad turns the JSR into a no-op load, so the collision routine is never entered. Companion code ALKUTGEL flips the following BCS at $b44e to BMI to keep the surrounding flow correct
$b56b: `and #$10` test of Entity_Active bit 4 inside the player-collision walker (Y=0..2). Game Genie OZVULSPX (Hit Anywhere / no-collision) overwrites the $29 opcode with $a9 (lda) so the AND becomes a load — the BNE following always sees nonzero and the JSR L_aa61 collision check is skipped
$f813: sister of $b56b in the broader entity-vs-entity collision pass: `and #$10` on Entity_Active,Y. Game Genie OZOYUEPX patches the $29 to $a9 (lda), short-circuiting the same collision check across more slots than OZVULSPX covers
$d309: `dec Rolls` instruction inside Roll_Attempt (.byte block). Game Genie SZESPUVK (Infinite Rolls) patches the $ce opcode at $d309 with $ad (lda), turning the decrement into a no-op. Confirms Roll_Attempt at $d303 spans through $d30b
$d2bc: immediate operand of `ora #$10` inside Fire_Handler at $d2bb that sets bit 4 of Button_DebounceFlags. Game Genie AEUSGZAP (Rapid Fire P1) patches the $10 to $00 so the debounce bit is never set — every frame counts as a fresh button-press, producing one bullet spawn per frame instead of one per 10 frames
$dc01: `lda #$ff / sta $0718` at the entry of one of the death/respawn handlers; sets the death-state byte $0718 to $ff. Game Genie PAEIXKNY patches the $ff immediate at $dc02 to $01, changing the value stored — likely the 'Easier Difficulty' cheat that downgrades the post-death state
$f828: `sta Entity_Active,Y` after `ora #$01` (sets bit 0 on the slot's active flag — likely the 'enemy is hit/dying' bit). Game Genie OLZNEE is a 6-letter unconditional poke that overwrites the $99 opcode with $b9 (lda), suppressing the store — enemies never have this bit set, which would change hit/death animation flow
$042e: set to $ff at $ac7e once the score crosses an extra-life threshold; a nonzero value suppresses re-checking the threshold (the bne $ac8e bypass at $ac72) so each crossing only awards one life. Cleared on game-init
$042f: high BCD digit of the running 'next extra-life' threshold. Bumped at $acbd via `lda $042f / clc / adc #$01` after each extra life is awarded — combined with the +8 carry into ExtraLife_NextThresh_Lo this raises the next threshold by 80,000 points
$0430: low BCD digit of the next-extra-life threshold; cleared by the threshold trigger at $ac85 then BCD-incremented by 8 at $acad each award (40 -> 48 wraps to 50, etc.). The first threshold (Score_D4 >= 2 -> 200,000) is hardcoded at $ac74 — these counters only steer subsequent extra lives
$ac1a: add to the player's BCD score and refresh the HUD. Inputs: $8a/$8b/$88/$89 hold the per-digit deltas to add into Score_D1/D2/D3/D4 (each 0..9). Score_D5 ($0427 / ActivePlayer_State) just propagates carry. Saves X/Y to $038b/$038c (which are otherwise general scratch), runs a 5-stage `cmp #$0a / sbc #$0a` BCD ripple across the digits, then JSRs HUD_Score_To_OAM at $ad39 and falls through into the extra-life check at $ac6f. Five known callers: $e046, $e096, $f8d3, $fa23, plus $f947 (in a .byte block)
$ac14: alt entry into Score_AddBCD that first zeros $88:$89 — call this when the score increment fits in two BCD digits (caller only sets $8a:$8b). Used by $d7df and $f947 (in .byte block)
$ac6f: fall-through tail of Score_AddBCD: if ExtraLife_PendingFlag is already set, skip; else if Score_D4 (10,000s) >= 2 (i.e. score crossed 200,000) award an extra life and arm the next threshold counter; finally drop into Score_Sync ($acc8). Reachable both via fallthrough and standalone call sites that just want the threshold/sync without an add
$acc8: compare TrueScore_Lo..Hi ($07bf..$07c4) digit-by-digit MSB-first against ActivePlayer_State..Score_D1 ($0427..$042b). If True < Display anywhere along the walk: branch to $ace0 (copy Display->True), then reload True from the per-player save buffer ($acf2) so a swap-in P2 doesn't clobber the unsaved P1 score. Returns by restoring caller's X/Y from $038b/$038c
$ace0: 6-byte copy loop: ActivePlayer_State..Score_D1 -> TrueScore_Lo..Hi. Reached from Score_Sync when the display has caught up past the saved true value
$acf2: after Score_CopyDispToTrue, reload TrueScore from P1_SavedState (Active_Player_Index = 0) or P2_SavedState (= 1). Means TrueScore is the *common-bus* score working register and the per-player save buffer holds the *durable* digits across player swaps
$ad0e: INC Lives_Ones; if it hits $0a wrap to 0 and INC Lives_Tens (capping at 9 to give the 99-life ceiling). Calls PPU_Queue_Refresh twice with $10 then $11 — the two HUD-refresh slots that redraw the lives digits
$ad39: copy the 6 BCD score digits into the score sprites at $020c. First syncs ActivePlayer_State..Score_D1 (low nibble masked) into the active player's save buffer ($044a P1 or $046b P2), then blasts a 24-byte sprite block from a per-player template ($ad99 for P1, $adb1 for P2) into OAM, and finally re-patches the 6 tile-id slots from the just-synced digits. Called from the tail of Score_AddBCD
$a06b: HUD/PPU tile-refresh dispatcher. Caller passes a 5-bit refresh-target id in A; the routine derives a PPU_WriteCount and PPU source pointer from a base table at $a08c indexed by (A & 7), enqueues the redraw via PPU_WriteBlock infrastructure. ~10+ call sites across HUD-update paths (lives, score, level indicators)
$abc2: turn A (score-value index) into a pointer at $abd0 + idx*4 stored in $8a:$8b. Used by the per-enemy-type score arms in the .byte block at $ab3c-$abbc — each arm matches an enemy type then `jmp Score_LookupValue` to load the matching 4-byte score-add packet
